data.content_ref: inline:{\"claim\": \"State elites co‑opted Sea Peoples as auxiliary forces; the ensuing reliance on foreign mercenaries fractured internal power structures.\", \"mechanism\": \"Geopolitical‑military destabilization through overextension of mercenary troops undermined central authority.\", \"scope\": {\"covers\": [\"Mycenaean Greece\",\"Hittite Anatolia\",\"Ugarit\",\"Levantine city‑states\"], \"excludes\": [\"Egypt\"]}, \"forbidden\": [{\"case\": \"Contemporary records exclusively describing Sea Peoples as external raiders with no evidence of formal alliance or service.\", \"eval\": \"rubric:std-hist\"}], \"prose_notes\": \"Analytic report format compares the varying degrees of Sea Peoples integration across the four polities, arguing that internal militarization, not external pressure alone, precipitated collapse.\"}
